description_long: This object consists of a pair of crossed swords with broad, curved blades and a distinctive hexagonal red pattern on one blade. The blades are metallic, with one side showing a weathered, matte steel finish and the other adorned with a striking red honeycomb design. The handles are wrapped in black material, likely leather or synthetic, and feature brown metallic guards with a simple, functional design. The swords are robust and appear to be designed for both display and practical use, with ergonomic grips for secure handling. The overall aesthetic is modern with a touch of fantasy, making them stand out in an urban or decorative context. No sheath or scabbard is present, and the swords are shown as a matched pair.
description: A pair of crossed, broad-bladed swords with one blade featuring a red hexagonal pattern and both having black-wrapped handles and brown guards.
description_view_0: Front view showing both swords crossed, highlighting the red hexagonal pattern on one blade and the weathered steel finish on the other.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the blades and the thickness of the handles, with the red pattern visible along the edge of one blade.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the crossed swords and the hexagonal pattern on the opposite side, showing the same handle and guard details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, again showing the slimness of the blades and the handle construction, with the red pattern on the other blade.
attribute_handle_design: black-wrapped, contoured, with brown metallic guard and ergonomic grip
attribute_ornamentation: red hexagonal pattern on one blade
